thread.title: Mayor Bowser and Marion Barry Jr.'s widow unveil mural honoring DC's 'Mayor for Life'
thread.title_full: Mayor Bowser and Marion Barry Jr.'s widow unveil mural honoring DC's 'Mayor for Life'

author: 7News Staff
title: Mayor Bowser and Marion Barry Jr.'s widow unveil mural honoring DC's 'Mayor for Life'
text: WASHINGTON (7News) — Former First Lady Cora Masters Barry was joined by Mayor Muriel Bowser on Saturday to reveal a mural honoring her late husband at the Marion S. Barry, Jr. Building. The mural was commissioned by the DC Commission on the Arts and Humanities (CAH), in partnership with the Department of General Services (DGS) and in collaboration with the Marion Barry Jr. Legacy Foundation. SEE ALSO | Marion Barry's wife requests meeting with &pizza, threatens lawsuit "My husband really loved Washington, DC, and its residents. The mural captures some of the major contributions he made to the City, and some of the people who worked with him to help build the City and empower its residents,” said Masters Barry. “I was thrilled that so many residents and visitors were able to join us on this inspirational, entertaining, educational, and historical day, as we honored a man, Marion Barry Jr., whose legacy and love of this city was so deep and strong.” Nabeeh “Nabi” Bilal, co-founder of CreativeJunkFood, LLC, designed the mural. It consists of three panels titled Big Vision, Big Impact, and Big Legacy, inspired by Mayor Barry’s philosophy of pursuing “Big Dreams and Big Goals.” SEE ALSO | Is Mayor Muriel Bowser violating DC Code by accepting lavish trips? | I-Team “Mayor Barry taught us the importance of having a big vision, and I’m grateful for all the people in our city who are working with us every day to keep his vision alive,” said Mayor Bowser. “He also taught us what it means to be a fighter. And what we’re fighting for is the vision that Mr. Barry had – a vision centered around opportunity, justice, dignity and an unwavering belief in the power of the people.”
